BCEAO Côte d'Ivoire Canary — Q3 2026 Intelligence Brief

CIRI History — Jan to Jul 2026
KC_02 Cocoa Supply Shock — UNIQUE KILL CONDITION (weight=0.35)
Why KC_02 is Ivory Coast's Defining Risk — The 40% Global Cocoa Lock

Côte d'Ivoire produces 40%+ of global cocoa (2.2M tonnes per year). This is not just an export risk — it is a single-country supply lock on a globally consumed commodity. In 2024, cocoa prices surged above $10,000/mt (record high), proving the price shock mechanism fires. This KC_02 signal is invisible in other West Africa canaries — no other country controls this share.

The dual-direction risk:

  • Upward shock (>$8,000/mt): Chocolate makers HSY, MDLZ, MKC face margin collapse. Hershey warned on earnings in 2024. Cocoa is ~35% of Hershey's COGS.
  • Downward shock (<$2,000/mt): Revenue collapse for Ivory Coast government and 1+ million cocoa farmers. Fiscal crisis → IMF programme breach → KC_01 cascade.
  • Crop failure: Black pod disease (Phytophthora megakarya) threatens the western cocoa belt. El Niño conditions amplify disease spread.

NIB (iPath Cocoa ETN, Bloomberg: NIB): This ETN tracks cocoa futures directly. When cocoa price spikes, NIB rises — it is inverse to chocolate makers. Correctly positioning: BEARISH HSY/MDLZ/MKC + BULLISH NIB during cocoa crisis.

NIB SPECIAL NOTE: iPath Bloomberg Cocoa Subindex Total Return ETN (NIB) tracks cocoa futures. Cocoa price spike → NIB RISES. HSY/MDLZ/MKC simultaneously BEARISH. This inverse relationship is captured in the KC_02 channel: direction=+1 for NIB, -1 for chocolate makers.
PeriodStatusPrice LevelKey Context
2020-2021 Stable $2,300–2,700/mt Pandemic disruption minor; La Niña year
2022 Mild stress $2,600–2,900/mt Rising freight costs; climate uncertainty
2023 Moderate $3,000–4,000/mt El Niño warning; Black pod disease emergence
Jan 2024 CRISIS $4,500→10,200/mt Historic spike; Hershey issues profit warning
Apr 2024 RECORD HIGH >$10,000/mt KC_02 mechanism FIRES; HSY/MDLZ earnings cut
H2 2024 Correction $8,000–6,500/mt Partial recovery; supply disruption persists
2025 Elevated $5,500–6,200/mt Above pre-crisis; farmers slow replanting
Q3 2026 WATCH ~$5,200/mt CIRI 0.3850 WATCH; harvest pressure monitoring
Q3 2026 Status: WATCH — Moderate harvest pressure; El Niño residual; Conseil Café-Cacao monitoring. CIRI cocoa component: KC_02 = 0.45 WATCH.
CIRI Kill Conditions — Full Breakdown
KC Description Weight Score Zone CIRI Contrib.
KC_01 IMF Programme Breach — WAEMU fiscal criteria breach; CFA franc zone sustainability; programme suspension signals. Q3 2026: IMF on track, WAEMU targets met. 25% 0.2000 NORMAL 0.0500
KC_02 Cocoa Supply Shock — UNIQUE KILL CONDITION. 40%+ global cocoa (2.2M tonnes/yr). 2024 record >$10,000/mt proved mechanism. HSY/MDLZ/MKC exposed. NIB (cocoa ETN) rises on shock. IVORY COAST UNIQUE 35% 0.4500 WATCH 0.1575
KC_03 Political/Military Coup Risk — West Africa coup contagion (Mali 2021, Burkina 2022, Niger 2023). Ouattara succession post-2025. Gbagbo/Ouattara reconciliation fragility. 25% 0.2500 WATCH 0.0625
KC_04 Port of Abidjan Disruption — West Africa's largest container port; hub for 6 landlocked Sahel countries (Mali, Burkina, Niger, Chad, CAR, S. Sudan). 10% 0.1000 NORMAL 0.0100
KC_05 French Military Withdrawal Risk — ~800 troops in Abidjan. Unlike Mali/Niger/Burkina (expelled France), Ivory Coast still hosts French forces. 5% 0.1000 NORMAL 0.0050
Cocoa Economy Intelligence — Production, Price & GDP
Production Volume
2.2M t/yr
Peak production; 40%+ global share. Conseil Café-Cacao controls export pricing.
2024 Record Price
>$10,000/mt
KC_02 mechanism proved. Hershey profit warning Q1 2024. Historic high.
Q3 2026 Price
~$5,200/mt
Above pre-crisis baseline. WATCH zone. El Niño residual harvest pressure.
Revenue Collapse Threshold
<$2,000/mt
Below this = Ivory Coast government fiscal crisis + farmer income collapse.
West Africa Geopolitics — Coup Contagion & Port of Abidjan
Ivory Coast — The Anchor State Holding
Fell to coups:
  • Mali — August 2021
  • Burkina Faso — Jan 2022 + Sep 2022
  • Niger — July 2023
  • Guinea — September 2021
  • Gabon — August 2023
Ivory Coast holding:
  • CIRI KC_03 = 0.25 (WATCH, not ELEVATED)
  • Ouattara succession = key 2025-2026 risk
  • Gbagbo 2010-2011 civil war scar persists
  • French ~800 troops still present
US Stock Transmission Channels — 9 Equities
Transmission Channel Weights
ChannelDescriptionWeightScore
cocoa_supply_shock Côte d'Ivoire crop failure or price spike → HSY/MDLZ/MKC margin squeeze. NIB (cocoa ETN) RISES when cocoa spikes (inverse to chocolate makers). 45% 0.4020
political_instability Coup risk → West Africa EM risk premium + port disruption → SCGLY/BNPQY French banks bearish; AMKBY (Maersk) shipping routes disrupted. 35% 0.2470
imf_waemu_stress IMF programme breach → WAEMU fiscal credibility → CFA franc peg questions → SCGLY/BNPQY sovereign risk on CFA-zone loan books. 20% 0.2320
Equity Impact Table
Ticker Name Direction Severity Transmission Note
HSY Hershey BEARISH HIGH 40% cocoa cost input; direct margin squeeze; cocoa shock = EPS cut
MDLZ Mondelez BEARISH HIGH Cadbury/Oreo cocoa intensive; major input cost; price spike = BEARISH
MKC McCormick BEARISH MODERATE Cocoa/food ingredients; slight bearish on supply shock
NIB iPath Cocoa ETN BULLISH [COCOA ETN RISES] HIGH INVERSE: Cocoa ETN RISES when cocoa price spikes — NOT bearish
ADM Archer-Daniels BEARISH MODERATE Cocoa processing; supply disruption = volume loss = BEARISH
SCGLY Société Générale ADR BEARISH HIGH French bank; big Ivory Coast/CFA zone exposure; IMF breach risk
BNPQY BNP Paribas ADR BEARISH HIGH French bank; CFA zone lending; WAEMU sovereign bond risk
AMKBY Maersk ADR BEARISH MODERATE West Africa shipping; Abidjan hub disruption; 6-country trade routes
OLMIY Olam International BEARISH HIGH Cocoa trader; Ivory Coast operations; both cocoa + political channels
NIB INVERSE LOGIC: When KC_02 fires (cocoa supply shock), HSY/MDLZ/MKC are BEARISH (input cost squeeze) but NIB (iPath Cocoa ETN) is BULLISH — it tracks cocoa futures price directly. A portfolio long NIB + short HSY captures the KC_02 spread.

cacao Côte d'Ivoire · récolte cacao · fève cacao Abidjan · Conseil Café Cacao · maladie cacaoyer · GEPEX cacao

coup d'état Côte · Ouattara succession · CEDEAO militaire · crise politique Abidjan · groupe armé Côte

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.1400  NORMAL

BCEAO / Côte d'Ivoire operates a frontier, structurally shallow supply chain, heavily dependent on a small number of entry points with minimal alternative routing — any chokepoint disruption has outsized domestic impact. No major maritime chokepoint exposure identified in structural config.

Critical import dependencies include refined fuels, food commodities, industrial machinery, consumer electronics. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Primary exports — cocoa, coffee, cashews, rubber — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.1400 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0500  NORMAL

BCEAO / Côte d'Ivoire is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a frontier market, West Africa real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in West Africa are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0500 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3200  WATCH

BCEAO / Côte d'Ivoire faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Drought Heatwave, Flood.

The insurance market in West Africa is minimal, with negligible domestic insurance infrastructure — catastrophic events rely almost entirely on international reinsurance or sovereign guarantees. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.3200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Drought Heatwave event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_IN01 Natural Catastrophe35%0.1500NORMAL
KC_IN02 Reinsurance Market25%0.3200WATCH
KC_IN03 Property Ins. Withdrawal20%0.3500WATCH

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.1400  NORMAL

BCEAO / Côte d'Ivoire operates a frontier, structurally shallow supply chain, heavily dependent on a small number of entry points with minimal alternative routing — any chokepoint disruption has outsized domestic impact. No major maritime chokepoint exposure identified in structural config.

Critical import dependencies include refined fuels, food commodities, industrial machinery, consumer electronics. Dual vulnerability to both energy price shocks and semiconductor supply disruptions creates compounding risk vectors.

Primary exports — cocoa, coffee, cashews, rubber — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.1400 NORMAL — Supply chain channels are operating within baseline parameters. No structural disruption signals active at this time.

Watch signals: SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.0500  NORMAL

BCEAO / Côte d'Ivoire is classified as a frontier-market real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a frontier market, West Africa real estate operates with limited pricing transparency, thin transaction volumes, and minimal mortgage market depth. Property rights frameworks may be underdeveloped, creating additional legal risk for international investors.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in West Africa are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.0500 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger  |  Capital outflow episode → property market freeze and liquidity withdrawal

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3200  WATCH

BCEAO / Côte d'Ivoire faces 2 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Drought Heatwave, Flood.

The insurance market in West Africa is minimal, with negligible domestic insurance infrastructure — catastrophic events rely almost entirely on international reinsurance or sovereign guarantees. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 3 — high climate vulnerability; physical and transition risks are material and accelerating. Physical climate risks — including intensifying storm frequency, sea level rise, and heat stress on agriculture — are expected to materially increase insured losses over the medium term, potentially triggering insurer withdrawal from high-risk zones and creating insurance coverage gaps.

INRI 0.3200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Drought Heatwave event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Insurer non-renewal notices in high-risk zones → coverage gap emergence  |  Sovereign disaster declaration without insurance backstop → fiscal cliff  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress
